body.label: Definition 1
body.definition: Let G be a group with identity element e. A function l : G -> R is a pseudo-length function on G if the following four conditions hold: nonnegativity, identity, symmetry, and triangle inequality.
body.name: PseudoLength

body.text: The nonnegativity condition is included explicitly so that the codomain can be the usual real numbers in a formalization. Equivalently, one may take the codomain to be the set of nonnegative real numbers and omit the separate nonnegativity axiom.

body.label: Definition 2
body.definition: Let G be a group with identity element e. A pseudo-length function l : G -> R is a length function if, for every g in G, g != e implies 0 < l(g).
body.name: IsLength

body.label: Definition 3
body.definition: Let G be a group. A pseudo-length function l : G -> R is homogeneous if, for every g in G and every integer n in Z, one has l(g^n)=|n| l(g).
body.name: IsHomogeneousPseudoLength

body.text: In particular, for every g in G, homogeneity gives l(g^2)=2l(g).

proof.text: Fix x,y in G. First prove l(yxy^{-1}) <= l(x). For a positive integer n, homogeneity gives n l(yxy^{-1}) = l((yxy^{-1})^n). Since (yxy^{-1})^n = yx^n y^{-1}, the triangle inequality gives l(yx^n y^{-1}) <= l(y)+l(x^n)+l(y^{-1}). Using symmetry and homogeneity, n l(yxy^{-1}) <= 2l(y)+n l(x), hence l(yxy^{-1}) <= l(x)+2l(y)/n. Letting n tend to infinity gives l(yxy^{-1}) <= l(x). proof.text: By Lemma 9, l factors through a homogeneous pseudo-length function barL : G_ab -> R. By Lemma 11, barL factors through the torsion-free quotient A:=G_ab/T(G_ab); let tildeL : A -> R be the induced homogeneous pseudo-length function. By Lemma 12, tildeL extends to a seminorm on VQ:=A tensor_Z Q. By Lemma 13, there exist a real Banach space B and a canonical map VQ -> B preserving the seminorm. Define phi:G -> B to be the composite G -> G_ab -> A -> VQ -> B. Each arrow is a group homomorphism when B is regarded additively, so phi is a group homomorphism. By construction l(g)=||phi(g)||_B for every g. If l is a length function and phi(g)=0, then l(g)=||phi(g)||_B=0; hence g=e. Thus phi is injective.

proof_steps.text: Injectivity and the norm give l(g)>0 for g != 0, and the norm axioms give l(0)=0, l(-g)=l(g), l(g+h)<=l(g)+l(h), and l(ng)=|n|l(g). Thus l is a homogeneous length function.
